title: Web
summary: Production-grade web design guidance for distinctive, polished interfaces. Use for visual direction, layout, typography, color, motion, component polish, accessibility, and anti-generic AI design rules.
use_when: Building or redesigning a website, landing page, dashboard, app screen, component, marketing page, admin UI, or design system.
Improving frontend polish, visual hierarchy, layout, typography, motion, responsiveness, or perceived product quality.
Do not use for general JS/TS implementation unless the task explicitly involves visual design or UI quality.

title: TypeScript + JavaScript
summary: Practical TypeScript/JavaScript engineering guidance for React, Next.js, Node.js, Vite, APIs, packages, and app code. Focuses on type safety, framework boundaries, async correctness, package discipline, verification, and common AI coding mistakes.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves package.json, tsconfig/jsconfig, .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx files, Node.js, React, Next.js, Vite, or common JS/TS frameworks and tooling.
Use for implementation, bug fixes, refactors, tests, API handlers, frontend behavior, build tooling, package work, or TypeScript errors in JS/TS projects.
Do not use merely because the task is web-related; use the Web skill only when the task requires visual design, layout polish, UI aesthetics, or premium frontend presentation.

title: Python
summary: Practical Python engineering guidance for APIs, automation, AI/ML, data scripts, CLIs, async services, and 2D/3D game/tool prototypes. Focuses on environment discipline, validation, model-loading patterns, async correctness, safe file/process handling, and common AI coding mistakes.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves .py/.pyi/.ipynb files, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, uv/poetry/pip/conda files, Python APIs, scripts, automation, data/ML pipelines, or Python game/tool code.

Use for implementation, bug fixes, refactors, tests, packaging, FastAPI/Flask/Django endpoints, AI/ML inference/training code, pandas/NumPy data work, CLIs, async services, pygame/arcade/pyglet/Panda3D/Ursina/Blender Python work.
Do not use for non-Python repos merely because Python exists in tooling, CI, or generated scripts; use only when Python code or Python runtime behavior is part of the task.

title: C / C++ Native
summary: Practical C/C++ native engineering guidance for CMake/Make/MSVC/Clang/GCC projects, low-level libraries, engine/plugin code, memory ownership, headers, linking, ABI, concurrency, performance, and native security pitfalls.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves .c/.h/.cpp/.hpp/.cc/.cxx files, CMakeLists.txt, Makefile, Meson/Bazel/Premake, native libraries, compiler/linker errors, engine modules, plugins, FFI/bindings, or low-level/performance-sensitive native code.

Use for C/C++ implementation, bug fixes, refactors, build-system fixes, memory/lifetime issues, header/API work, linker errors, ABI/platform issues, native game/engine code, and security-sensitive parsers or file/network handling.
Do not use for C#/.NET projects; use csharp-dotnet when the task involves .cs, .csproj, .sln managed code, ASP.NET, Unity C#, NuGet, or .NET runtime behavior.

title: C# / .NET
summary: Practical C#/.NET engineering guidance for ASP.NET Core, EF Core, NuGet/MSBuild, async/await, nullable reference types, DI, Unity C#, desktop apps, libraries, APIs, testing, and common managed-code mistakes.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves .cs files, .csproj/.sln, Program.cs/Startup.cs, appsettings.json, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, NuGet, MSBuild, Unity C# scripts, MAUI, WPF, WinForms, Blazor, or .NET services/libraries.

Use for C# implementation, bug fixes, refactors, API handlers, EF queries/migrations, async/await behavior, DI registration, package/project-file changes, Unity gameplay scripts, desktop app code, and .NET test/build issues.
Do not use for native C/C++ work; use cpp-native when the task involves .c/.cpp/.h/.hpp files, CMake, compiler/linker errors, native libraries, or ABI/platform issues.

title: SQL / Database
summary: Practical SQL/database guidance for schemas, migrations, queries, indexes, transactions, ORM pitfalls, tenant scoping, pagination, and durable data changes. Focuses on safe persistence work and common agent mistakes around data access.
use_when: Use only when the task touches .sql files, migrations, schemas, database models, ORM queries, indexes, transactions, data integrity, query performance, pagination, or persistent application data behavior.

Use for PostgreSQL, MySQL/MariaDB, SQLite, SQL Server, Prisma, Drizzle, TypeORM, Sequelize, Knex, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, Django ORM, Laravel migrations/Eloquent, EF Core migrations/LINQ queries, or similar database work.
Do not use for generic backend changes merely because the app has a database dependency; activate only when database structure, queries, data access, or persistence semantics are part of the work.

title: Shell / DevOps
summary: Practical shell and DevOps guidance for scripts, Docker, systemd, reverse proxies, CI/CD, environment variables, secrets, logs, permissions, ports, and deployment/runtime debugging. Focuses on safe operational changes and verification.
use_when: Use only when the task touches .sh/.bash/.zsh/.ps1 scripts, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, CI config, GitHub Actions, systemd units, Nginx/Apache configs, deployment scripts, cron jobs, SSH/SCP, Linux services, ports, logs, permissions, or environment variables.
Use for runtime/deployment failures, service startup, reverse proxy/TLS issues, containerization, CI failures, production config, shell automation, and operational verification commands.
Do not use for normal application implementation unless the current issue is deployment, runtime, shell, infrastructure, or tooling related.

title: Java / Kotlin
summary: Practical Java/Kotlin JVM guidance for Spring Boot, Android, Maven/Gradle, JPA/Hibernate, REST APIs, dependency injection, nullability, coroutines, testing, and common JVM agent mistakes.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves .java/.kt/.kts files, pom.xml, build.gradle/build.gradle.kts, Gradle/Maven wrappers, Spring Boot, Android, JPA/Hibernate, JVM tests, or JVM stack traces/build errors.
Use for Java/Kotlin implementation, bug fixes, API handlers, services/repositories, DTO/entity mapping, build/dependency work, coroutine/reactive behavior, Android lifecycle code, and JVM tests.
Do not use for C#/.NET despite syntax similarities, and do not use for JavaScript/TypeScript even if the project uses Gradle/Vite tooling nearby.

title: Go
summary: Practical Go engineering guidance for modules, HTTP services, CLIs, context propagation, explicit error handling, goroutines/channels, database/sql, testing, formatting, and common Go agent mistakes.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves .go files, go.mod/go.sum/go.work, Go tests, Go CLI/server code, gRPC, Gin/Fiber/Chi/Echo, Cobra, database/sql, goroutines/channels, race conditions, or Go build/runtime errors.
Use for Go implementation, bug fixes, refactors, HTTP APIs, database access, concurrency, module/dependency changes, CLI tools, tests, formatting, and build issues.
Do not use for non-Go projects with unrelated Docker/tooling files, or when the word 'go' is only natural-language wording.

title: PHP / Laravel
summary: Practical PHP/Laravel guidance for raw PHP, Composer, Laravel, Eloquent, migrations, sessions/auth, PDO, Blade, WordPress-aware work, public_html hosting, Apache/PHP runtime issues, and common PHP security mistakes.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves .php files, composer.json/composer.lock, artisan, Laravel apps, WordPress plugin/theme files, Blade templates, PHP sessions/auth, PDO/MySQL, Apache .htaccess, public_html deployments, PHP-FPM, or PHP runtime errors.

Use for PHP/Laravel implementation, bug fixes, routes/controllers, validation, Eloquent queries, migrations, queues, Composer changes, file uploads, sessions, WordPress-aware code, and PHP hosting issues.
Do not use for JavaScript/CSS inside a PHP project unless PHP/backend behavior is part of the task.

title: Godot
summary: Practical Godot guidance for GDScript/C# projects, scenes/nodes/resources, 2D/3D gameplay, physics, UI, multiplayer, exports, performance, and common Godot agent mistakes.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves project.godot, .gd, .tscn, .tres, .res, Godot C# scripts, scenes, nodes, resources, signals, exports, physics, multiplayer, or Godot engine/runtime errors.

Use for Godot gameplay implementation, UI, scene architecture, input, physics, resource/data patterns, multiplayer authority/sync, performance, export/build issues, and GDScript/C# engine-specific fixes.
Do not use for generic game design without Godot implementation, or for Unity/Unreal projects.

title: Unity
summary: Practical Unity guidance for C# gameplay, MonoBehaviour lifecycle, prefabs/scenes, ScriptableObjects, physics, UI, packages, networking, editor tooling, performance, and common Unity agent mistakes.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves Unity project files such as Assets/, ProjectSettings/, Packages/manifest.json, .unity, .prefab, .asmdef, MonoBehaviour, ScriptableObject, Unity C# scripts, editor tooling, physics, UI, packages, or Unity build/runtime errors.

Use for Unity gameplay implementation, lifecycle bugs, prefab-safe C# changes, serialized fields, ScriptableObjects, physics/input/UI, networking authority, packages, editor tools, performance, and build issues.
Do not use for ordinary C#/.NET work unless Unity engine behavior is part of the task; use csharp-dotnet for non-Unity managed code.

title: Unreal Engine
summary: Practical Unreal Engine guidance for C++/Blueprint projects, actors/components, reflection macros, modules/build files, networking/replication, input, UI, assets, performance, and common Unreal agent mistakes.
use_when: Use only when the repository or task involves .uproject, .uplugin, Source/, .Build.cs, .Target.cs, UCLASS/USTRUCT/UPROPERTY/UFUNCTION, Blueprints, Actors, Components, replication, Gameplay Ability System, UMG/Slate, packaging, or Unreal build/runtime/editor errors.
Use for Unreal gameplay implementation, C++/Blueprint interoperability, UObject lifetime/GC, reflected APIs, module dependencies, replication, input, UI, assets, performance, and packaging issues.
Do not use for generic C++ unless Unreal engine APIs or project structure are involved; use cpp-native for non-Unreal native code.

title: Cybersecurity
summary: Defensive cybersecurity guidance for secure code review, threat modeling, vulnerability discovery, CVE-aware dependency checks, hardening, and fix-oriented white-hat analysis. Strictly defensive and remediation-focused.
use_when: Use only when the user explicitly asks for security review, vulnerability assessment, hardening, threat modeling, CVE/dependency review, abuse resistance, authentication/authorization checks, secrets handling, exposed service review, or secure implementation.

Also use when the current code touches high-risk surfaces: auth, sessions, permissions, payments, file upload/download, deserialization, templates, SQL/ORM queries, command execution, SSRF-capable outbound requests, webhooks, cryptography, API keys, admin panels, multi-tenant data, network services, or sandboxing.
Do not use for ordinary implementation just because every app has security concerns. Keep output defensive: evidence, impact, remediation, safe tests, and no weaponized exploit guidance.
